title: Array Index Out of Bounds Bug Fix: Missing Max

statement.markdown: You are given an integer array `nums` containing at least one element. Write a method that returns the maximum sum of a subarray with non-zero length in `nums`. If no such subarray exists, return 0.
statement.outputFormat: The maximum sum of a subarray in `nums` with non-zero length
statement.notes: You may assume that the array is not empty.

examples.explanation: The maximum sum of a subarray is 4
No subarray in the array has a non-zero length

hints.text: Start by initializing a `max_sum` variable to the smallest possible integer value.

editorial.title: Correct Solution: Kadane's Algorithm
editorial.intuition: Kadane's algorithm is a dynamic programming approach to find the maximum sum of a subarray in an array.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach with time complexity of O(n^3)
editorial.optimalSolution: Kadane's algorithm with time complexity of O(n)
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof using induction and mathematical arguments.
editorial.dryRun: Dry run of the correct code

starterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int maxSubarraySum(int nums[], int n);}
starterCode.java: public class Solution {public int maxSubarraySum(int[] nums, int n);}
starterCode.python: class Solution: def maxSubarraySum(self, nums: list[int]) -> int:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: int maxSubarraySum(int nums[], int n) { int max_sum = nums[0]; for(int i = 1; i < n; i++) { max_sum = max_sum + nums[i]; } return max_sum; }
buggyStarterCode.java: public int maxSubarraySum(int[] nums, int n) { int max_sum = nums[0]; for(int i = 1; i < n; i++) { max_sum = max_sum + nums[i]; } return max_sum; }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def maxSubarraySum(self, nums: list[int]) -> int: max_sum = nums[0]; for i in range(1, len(nums)): max_sum = max_sum + nums[i]; return max_sum;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function maxSubarraySum(nums) { let max_sum = nums[0]; for(let i = 1; i < nums.length; i++) { max_sum = max_sum + nums[i]; } return max_sum; };

functionSignature.java: public int maxSubarraySum(int[] nums, int n);

solutions.python: class Solution: def maxSubarraySum(self, nums: list[int]) -> int: max_sum = float('-inf'); current_sum = 0; for num in nums: current_sum = max(num, current_sum + num); max_sum = max(max_sum, current_sum); return max_sum if max_sum != float('-inf') else 0;
solutions.cpp: class Solution {public: int maxSubarraySum(int nums[], int n) { int max_sum = float('-INF'); int current_sum = 0; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { current_sum = max(nums[i], current_sum + nums[i]); max_sum = max(max_sum, current_sum); } return max_sum == float('-INF') ? 0 : max_sum; }}
